title: papaver pilosum subsp. strictum
intro: Papaver pilosum subsp. strictum, also known as the Himalayan poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family, characterized by its hairy stems and bright yellow to orange-red flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve coughs, due to its high concentration of alkaloids such as thebaine and papaverine. The health benefits of Papaver pilosum subsp. strictum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and even act as a natural analgesic. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
